---
species: "Fucus spiralis"
accepted_code: "FUSP"
alternate_codes: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
common_name: "Spiral Wrack"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Brown algae found in the upper-intertidal with no air bladders, heart-Shaped or globular receptacles, blades that rotate in spiral fashion" # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/fucus_spiralis_huxley_seaweedsofcanada.jpg'
image_credit: 'Image by Huxley from Seaweeds of Canada' # MUST have an image credit
categories:
  - brown algae
sampling_programs:
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
habitat:
  - intertidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300904
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145547
---


**Other images:** 

![Alt image of hiru](/images/fucus_spiralis_Marco_seaweedofcanada.jpg)
<br/>
Image showing receptacles with a ridge by Marco I. from [Seaweed of Canada](https://seaweedcanada.wordpress.com/fucus-spiralis-linnaeus-bc-ar-a/).

<br/>


**Distinguishing features:** No air bladders. Heart-Shaped or Globular receptacles. Receptacles are swollen, blunt at tip, less than 4 times as long as wide, WITH a distinct rim around the edge. Blades rotate in spiral fashion. Found in the upper intertidal. 


<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Fucus vessiculousus* (FUVE) and *Fucus distichus* (FUDI). FUVE has paired air bladders, FUDI has no air bladders. Both FUDI and FUVE do not typically twist. FUSP receptacles have a distinct rim around the edge. If unable to differentiate, use mark as *Fucus* Unknown (FUCU).

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145547)**
